title: hybanthus prunifolius
intro: Hybanthus prunifolius, also known as the poison apple or green passionflower, is a plant species in the family Gentianaceae. It is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hybanthus prunifolius include its ability to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Hybanthus prunifolius can be made by infusing the leaves and roots in water, or by using the plant's extract in teas, tinctures, or ointments.
